entry.title: Re: pyodbc runs "select" queries very slowly when providi...
entry.content: Thank you for the extensive comment! A lot to chew on there, I'm playing around with things now, but that last bit you said about "we remove the exec part of the query" is very interesting! It appears that, for a simple solution, one can just add DYNAMICPREPARE=1, autocommit=1, and remove 'exec' from their stored procedure calls, and everything works correctly (at least, in my tests so far). If none of the other ideas in this thread pan out for me, I can at least use that. Thank you!

entry.title: WalkMe Now Available in SAP SuccessFactors : A New Era of Digital Adoption
entry.content:
Supercharge Your SAP SuccessFactors ROI with Embedded WalkMe Support 😎

SAP SuccessFactors is taking a significant step forward in user experience and adoption by integrating WalkMe, an AI-powered digital adoption platform. This integration introduces free embedded smart walkthroughs and guided tours across various modules, delivering step-by-step assistance tailored to users in different roles. By making processes easier to navigate, these tools help improve user adoption, enhance productivity, and ensure organizations get the most out of their SAP SuccessFactors investment.

While implementing SAP SuccessFactors can be a powerful driver of HR transformation, bringing streamlined processes, improved user experiences, and rich, data-driven insights, true value is only achieved when users are fully engaged. That’s where WalkMe plays a vital role, guiding users through key tasks and empowering them to confidently use the system from day one.

 

Overview: What is WalkMe?

WalkMe is a digital adoption platform that overlays on enterprise software like SAP SuccessFactors to provide real-time, in-app guidance. It offers:

  • Step-by-step walkthroughs for completing tasks
  • Insights into user behavior and adoption
  • Automation for repetitive processes

 

For business leaders and administrators, this means better onboarding, fewer support tickets, and a faster return on investment. For end users, it means intuitive help when and where it’s needed.

 

What’s New in the 1H 2025 Update:

Embedded Smart Walkthroughs

SAP SuccessFactors now includes free, embedded WalkMe tours across several modules . These in-app tours support users in completing HR tasks like goal setting, giving feedback, updating profiles, and more, without needing to leave the system or search external help resources.

Expanded Library of Guided Tours

A wide selection of new guided tours is now available, tailored to different user roles - employees, managers, and admins. Each tour provides clear, contextual instructions on how to use a feature, helping to accelerate system adoption across your organization.

WalkMe Guided Tours: Available Tours and Use Cases

WalkMe now offers a variety of guided tours directly embedded within SAP SuccessFactors. These tours are designed for different user roles - employees, managers, and administrators, and provide step-by-step help for key tasks. Below is a summary of the available tours and what they cover:

  • Create Goals Using AI Tools (End Users)
    Learn how to create performance or development goals using generative AI capabilities. Start the tour from the Goal Creation Options page by selecting “Goals” from the main menu and then choosing “Create Goal.”
  • Track Your Team’s Career Growth (End Users)
    Understand how to track career development for your team, including aspirations and skill growth. Begin the tour from the Summary tab of the Team View page in the Growth Portfolio.
  • Give Feedback (End Users)
    Learn how to provide feedback to colleagues. This tour starts from the landing page of Continuous Feedback.

 

  • Request Feedback (End Users)
    Discover how to request feedback from others. Access this tour from the same Continuous Feedback landing page.

  • Remind Team of Overdue Courses (Managers)
    Managers can learn how to identify overdue learning and send tailored reminders to their teams.
    Start from the Learning Home Page in the new experience.

 

  • Explore the Company Org Chart (End Users)
    Learn how to navigate the company’s organizational structure. This tour can be accessed from any page within the system.

 

  • Enable the Latest People Profile (Administrators)
    Understand how to enable the Full Profile of the latest People Profile. This tour is available from any page in the system.

 

 

  • Configure the Latest People Profile (Administrators)
    Learn how to set up Profile Preview, Spotlight, and Full Profile. This is also available from any page.

 

  • Get to Know Your Public Profile (End Users)
    Discover how to use the Profile Preview and Spotlight, and how to update your public profile information. Accessible from any page in the system.

 

  • Get to Know Your Full Profile (End Users)
    Learn how to view different sections of your profile, use shortcuts for common tasks, and access your public profile. Start this tour from the Full Profile page of the latest People Profile.

 

  • Understand Your Compensation (End Users)
    Find out how to view a detailed breakdown of your compensation package. This tour starts from the Compensation category within the Full Profile of the latest People Profile.

 ⚠️ Note: In my demo instance, I couldn't test the following scenarios: Create Goals Using AI Tools (End Users) / Track Your Team’s Career Growth (End Users)

Configuration Requirements:

These WalkMe tours are managed by SAP SuccessFactors and are disabled by default. Next steps:

  • WalkMe is enabled in your SAP SuccessFactors environment.
  • The relevant SuccessFactors feature is turned on and properly configured.
  • Users have the correct permissions to access the applicable feature.

When these conditions are met, users will automatically see the available guided tours on supported pages—no additional user training needed.

How to Access the Tours?

Accessing a WalkMe tour is simple:

  • Look for the WalkMe icon (?) in the global header of your SAP SuccessFactors instance.
  • Click the icon to view available tours for the page you’re on.

 WalkMe intelligently filters and displays tours that are relevant to your current task or screen, reducing confusion and boosting productivity.

Why It Matters

The enhanced WalkMe integration offers tangible benefits to both users and system administrators:

  • Accelerated adoption of new and existing features
  • Reduced support queries thanks to in-system help
  • Improved data accuracy and compliance
  • Faster onboarding and change management

By guiding users in real time, WalkMe enables them to complete processes confidently and consistently—ultimately maximizing the value of your SAP SuccessFactors investment.

Final Thoughts

As digital transformation accelerates, user experience is more important than ever. WalkMe’s integration with SAP SuccessFactors is a game-changer for organizations seeking to drive adoption, increase self-sufficiency, and ensure long-term success with their HR systems.

Whether you're just starting your journey or looking to improve adoption across the board, enabling these new guided tours is a smart and strategic step.

SAP SuccessFactors Just Got Smarter—Thanks to WalkMe!

Have you tried the new guided tours yet? Share your experiences or tips in the comments below!

entry.title: Why You Should Avoid Directly Creating VA01 Sales Orders and Embrace SAP CPQ - Improve Sales Process
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business environment, efficiency and accuracy are paramount. One area where companies often struggle is in your sales order processes. Traditionally, many organizations have relied on directly creating sales orders using VA01 in SAP ERP or S4 HANA. While this method is straightforward, it can lead to inefficiencies and errors. Instead, leveraging a hybrid solution via SAP CPQ (Configure, Price, Quote) can significantly streamline the Quote to Cash or Lead to Cash processes and available with SAP Standard Integrations offerings.

The Limitations of Directly Creating VA01 Sales Orders in ERP, S/4 HANA

Creating sales orders directly using VA01 involves manually entering data, which can be time-consuming and prone to errors. This method often lacks integration with other systems, leading to discrepancies in pricing, product configurations, and customer information, profitability calculations, guardrails, negotiations & output document acceptance from customer.

Additionally, it does not provide a guided sales process, which can result in inconsistent handling of sales opportunities .

The Advantages of Using SAP CPQ

SAP CPQ offers a robust solution to these challenges by providing a centralized platform for managing the entire sales process, from quoting to order fulfillment.

Here are some key benefits:

  1. Increased Efficiency and Productivity: SAP CPQ streamlines the sales process by eliminating manual data entry, saving time and increasing productivity .
  2. Improved Quote Accuracy: Integration with product master data ensures that all quotes are accurate and up-to-date, reducing errors and improving customer satisfaction .
  3. Shorter Sales Cycles: The guided sales process helps sales representatives close deals more quickly, shortening sales cycles and boosting revenue generation .
  4. Enhanced Customer Experience: SAP CPQ provides a self-service portal for configuring products, requesting quotes, and tracking orders, improving the overall customer experience.

SAP CPQ - Business Process flow for Back-end Integ... - SAP Community

Comprehensive Integration Capabilities

SAP CPQ enables seamless integration with various systems, enhancing the overall sales process:

  • CRM Integration: SAP CPQ integrates with your CRM systems, allowing opportunities to be converted into quotes effortlessly.
  • SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing: Integration with SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing simplifies complex product configurations and ensures accurate pricing for the cart in real-time .
  • Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM): SAP CPQ integrates with CLM systems, streamlining the contract creation and approval process .
  • Output Documents and Approvals: Automated generation of output documents and streamlined approval workflows ensure consistency and compliance .
  • Standard Integration Packages via CPI: SAP CPQ offers standard integration packages via SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) for sales orders, solution quotations, and solution orders

SAP CPQ - Business Process flow for Back-end Integ... - SAP Community

How SAP CPQ Integrates with SAP S/4HANA

SAP CPQ seamlessly integrates with SAP S/4HANA, enabling the transfer of pricing and product configuration data, and master data replication

This integration ensures that all sales information is accurate and up-to-date, providing a consistent and efficient sales process.

Business Process Flow Diagram

Architecture Views | Solution Business for Hybrid Deployment | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Architecture Views | Lead to Cash for Hybrid Deployment | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Architecture Views | Network-Based Sales Processing for Hybrid Deployment | SAP Business Accelerator Hub


SAP CPQ - SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud Integration

Conclusion

By avoiding the direct creation of VA01 sales orders and adopting a hybrid solution via SAP CPQ, organizations can significantly enhance their sales efficiency, accuracy, and customer satisfaction. Embracing this modern approach not only streamlines the sales process but also positions businesses for greater success in today's competitive landscape. Also it ensures you have clear auditing track of the entire Quote to Cash process been automated end to end.

Are you ready to transform your sales process with SAP CPQ? Let's start the journey towards a more efficient and productive sales operation!

entry.title: Re: pyodbc runs "select" queries very slowly when providi...
entry.content: One other note for investigation purposes: you noticed that the "SELECT" statement didn't perform differently between your two DYNAMICPREPARE=1 tests. Well, I've found in some extra testing that this difference only exists when querying large tables (mine has 83 million rows), which might make a difference. My table *has* an index on the column I'm querying, and running "set showplan on" before my query reveals that the reason why it runs more slowly is because the plan says "Positioning at index start", whereas running the same query in sqsh says "Positioning by key". To be clear, the slow-running "SELECT" query *does* use the index, which really surprised me! I'm not too familiar with what that "positioning" statement means, but the plans are otherwise identical. I don't really know what to make of this, but it could be useful for further investigation

entry.title: Re: ASE 15.5 Poor performance from ODBC
entry.content:
For anyone visiting this page in the future, this thread is related: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/pyodbc-runs-quot-select-quot-queries-very-slowly-when-providing-parameter/qaq-p/14075794

The summary of the thread is:

  1. The "DYNAMICPREPARE=1" solution suggested in *this* thread is very useful, and it speeds up certain "SELECT" queries that run very slowly through pyodbc
  2. However, adding "DYNAMICPREPARE=1" can break certain stored procedures, ran using the syntax "exec[ute] db_name..proc_name @Param=?" through pyodbc
  3. One useful (and apparently stable) solution to the problem described in point 2 is to just remove the word "exec" from the string. E.g. this code is working for me: cursor.execute("db_name..proc_name@param_name=?", [parameter1])

To phrase things even shorter, if you're having a performance problem with pyodbc, do these two/three things to your code, which appears to fix it:

  1. Add "DYNAMICPREPARE=1" to your connection string
  2. Add autocommit=True to your connection (optional)
  3. Remove the words "exec" or "execute" from any stored proc calls your code makes

There is not a guaranteed 1:1 equivalency with OnPrem or Private.

You need to start with the requirement, then look into the best practices in the Process Navigator

entry.title: Re: Sales Order block for dangerous materials

entry.title: Re: pyodbc runs "select" queries very slowly when providi...
entry.content:
runs more slowly is because the plan says "Positioning at index start", whereas running the same query in sqsh says "Positioning by key".

Positioning by key means the index was used to quickly zero in on the desired row(s) based on a distinct search value (eg, key='abc').

Positioning at index start implies an index scan (as opposed to a table scan) and will occur when (in this case) the only search clause is on the index key but the search value is not a distinct value (eg, key like '%abc%', or key=substring('abc',1,3)).  In this situation ASE is left with scanning the entire table and applying the search clause against every row; if there's an index on the search column and ASE determines the index resides on fewer pages than the raw data then it will opt for an index scan.  In your case ASE is opting to scan the index (fewer pages than scanning the entire table) while applying the search clause against every key value in the index ... this will (usually) be faster than a full table scan but it will still be quite a bit slower than a query that can benefit from Positioning by key.

Why is ASE opting for an index scan? This would require knowing what exactly ASE is seeing as the search value (submitted by your python script).  This is where the previous comment about the DBA and MDA tables comes into play ... have the DBA capture the actual SQL being submitted by your application.  [Or use the trace method provided by Ryan.] Having the actual (likely mangled) search value in hand should get you a step closer to determining if there's an issue with the way you're constructing the query or an issue with the library you're using.

Does the 'slow' python query eventually return the same results as the sqsh query? or does it return no results?

entry.title: Re: pyodbc runs "select" queries very slowly when providi...
entry.content: My guess is there is a difference in the TDS sent to the ASE database. Maybe one does Named Parameters and one does ? Could be that the TDS token itself is Dynamic or RPC for one and not the other. Not sure if that is a framework thing, but in ASE it may be picking a different path based on this. Looking up sqsh it looks like an Open Client application and pyodbc is ODBC driver. I showed the TDS trace above for the other example. You can do this by adding this connection property for ODBC: protocolcapture=/some/location/cap /some/location = directory this will be writing to. cap = the prefix for the file going to be generated per connection. Run the test and then translate the file to text. cd $SYBASE/jutils-3_0/ribo setenv RIBO_HOME $SYBASE/jutils-3_0/ribo setenv JAVA_HOME $SYBASE/jre64 mv cap* $RIBO_HOME ./Ribo capname capname.txt Then vi the text file and search for your query. Open Client: setenv SYBOCS_DEBUG_FLAGS CS_DBG_PROTOCOL sqsh or isql It should generate a file in your execution directory $ cd $SYBASE $ source SYBASE.csh $ setenv SYBOCS_DEBUG_FLAGS CS_DBG_PROTOCOL $ isql -Usa -Psybase -Sccus 1> exit $ ls cap* captureWnc9fM

entry.title: Re: Building an Analytical List Page with CAP OData V4
entry.content:
using { CatalogService } from './service';

// 1. Aggregation and Analytical Annotations
annotate CatalogService.EmployeesAnalytics with @(
  Aggregation.ApplySupported: {
    Transformations: [
      'aggregate', 'groupby'
    ],
    GroupableProperties: [
      year, name
    ],
    AggregatableProperties: [{
      $Type: 'Aggregation.AggregatablePropertyType',
      Property: salary
    }]
  },
  Analytics.AggregatedProperty #totalSalary: {
    $Type : 'Analytics.AggregatedPropertyType',
    AggregatableProperty : salary,
    AggregationMethod : 'sum',
    Name : 'totalSalary',
    ![@Common.Label]: 'Total Salary'
  },
  Aggregation.CustomAggregate #salary: 'Edm.Int32'
){
  salary @analytics.Measure @Aggregation.default: #SUM;
};

// 2. Main Chart
annotate CatalogService.EmployeesAnalytics with @(
  UI.Chart: {
    $Type : 'UI.ChartDefinitionType',
    Title: 'Employee Salary by Year',
    ChartType : #Column,
    Dimensions: [year],
    DimensionAttributes: [{
      $Type : 'UI.ChartDimensionAttributeType',
      Dimension: year,
      Role: #Category
    }],
    DynamicMeasures: [
      ![@Analytics.AggregatedProperty#totalSalary]
    ],
    MeasureAttributes: [{
      $Type: 'UI.ChartMeasureAttributeType',
      DynamicMeasure: ![@Analytics.AggregatedProperty#totalSalary],
      Role: #Axis1
    }]
  },
  UI.PresentationVariant: {
    $Type: 'UI.PresentationVariantType',
    Visualizations: ['@UI.Chart']
  }
);

// 3. Selection Fields and Line Item
annotate CatalogService.EmployeesAnalytics with @(
  UI: {
    SelectionFields: [year],
    LineItem: [
      { $Type : 'UI.DataField', Value : ID },
      { $Type : 'UI.DataField', Value : name },
      { $Type : 'UI.DataField', Value : salary },
      { $Type : 'UI.DataField', Value : year }
    ]
  }
);

entry.title: Re: add a field when the activity assigned to an external...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

There are multiple ways to do this :

1. Use ActivityAssignedEvent (Refer: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIELD_SERVICE_MANAGEMENT/fsm_plan_dispatch/planning-domain-events.html?locale=en-US) 

2. If you want to just update a UDF on 'assign' of an activity to an external technician, then you can  check 'On Update' for re-assignments and 'On Create' for 1st time assignments. You would have to run this business rule on the ServiceAssignment Object and write a query to check whether the SA object has an external technician through a join on the Person object (externalResource field in Person object).

3. In case you are dealing with Crowd ecosystem, on simple assignment, you still would need to use options 1 or 2. But, if you want to do any action after dispatcher clicks on 'Release' on the Planning board, you can then use 'ActivityConfirmedEvent' (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIELD_SERVICE_MANAGEMENT/fsm_crowd/crowd-business-event-list.html?locale=en-US&q=crowd%2520business%2520events)

entry.title: Building Intelligent Data Applications with SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine
entry.content:
Authored By: Vivek RR @Vivek-RR, Priya Ronald D'Costa @PriyaD  and Akanksha Kulkarni @AKulk 

As part of the QRC1 2025 release, SAP HANA Cloud has unveiled its latest multi-model feature—the Knowledge Graph Engine (KGE). We're thrilled to announce the publication of a discovery mission that explores this powerful new capability in depth.

What Our Discovery Mission Offers

Our mission provides a comprehensive look at the Knowledge Graph Engine's key features, demonstrating how to build end-to-end applications and solution for processing:

  • Unstructured data (such as documents)
  • Tabular data through the SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Engine
  • Integrations with various LLM platforms, including:
    • SAP GenAI Hub
    • AWS Bedrock (direct partner access)
    • Azure OpenAI
  • Prompt templates for generating SPARQL queries based on user prompts

We've included fully functional repositories that can be validated with Python scripts and a demo application. The content is designed to be plug-and-play—we provide both unstructured and tabular datasets, requiring you to simply replace the credentials for SAP HANA Cloud and API keys for your preferred LLM service. Once you grasp the fundamentals of SAP HANA Cloud KGE, you can easily extend or modify the code for your specific use cases.

Background Reading

Several blogs have already addressed the announcement of SAP HANA Cloud KGE. For those new to the topic, I recommend reviewing  @shabana 's blogs to understand:

  • The basics of SAP HANA Cloud KGE
  • The RDF framework we've adopted
  • Core concepts of SPARQL

Additionally,  @thomashammer 's blogs on the QRC1 2025 release of KGE provide valuable insights into the latest developments. Finally for early adoption of KGE, please refer to @susenpoppe  blog.

About Our Discovery Mission

Our newly launched SAP Discovery Mission covers:

  1. The fundamentals of SAP HANA KGE
  2. Using graph transformers to process unstructured data and convert it to triples
  3. Creating ontologies for structured data and generating triples based on these ontologies

All repositories are provided as part of the mission, which you can access here:  Building Intelligent Data Applications with SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graphs

What is the Ideal Use Case for this Discovery Mission?

Consider an organization where project members must review numerous specification documents during onboarding. Instead of the time-consuming process of reviewing all documents manually, users could query document content using natural language, significantly reducing review time. This is precisely where having documents stored as triples in SAP HANA Cloud KGE delivers tremendous value.

In our demo scenario, we process a PDF document (SAP FAQ Note) through a graph transformer to extract its content. The extracted information is then processed using RDF libraries to convert it into triples. Finally, we implement a prompt template mechanism for intelligent retrieval based on business users' natural language queries.

For example, based on an FAQ note about SAP HANA hotspots, if a business user asks, "What is the HANA KPI collector?", our application:

  • Converts this natural language prompt into a precise SPARQL query using a prompting template
  • Executes the query against the knowledge graph
  • Delivers a refined, contextual response powered by AWS Bedrock foundation models like Anthropic Claude Sonnet

This scenario clearly demonstrates how our solution:

  1. Chunks unstructured documents (PDFs, Word files) into manageable segments
  2. Employs graph transformers to analyze these chunks and identify nodes and edges
  3. Transforms graph transformer responses into standardized RDF triples
  4. Ingests these RDF triples into SAP HANA Cloud using the Knowledge Graph Engine via SPARQL
  5. Converts business user prompts into executable SPARQL queries through specialized prompting templates
  6. Routes retrieved triples to an LLM to generate concise, accurate responses for the user

While we use SAP FAQ notes rather than actual customer data (for privacy reasons), this approach demonstrates all the core capabilities mentioned above. Our Discovery Mission includes a comprehensive schema for tabular data applications, and we'll share additional scenarios and code snippets in upcoming blog posts.

Another powerful use case involves business users interacting with tabular data through natural language rather than pre-built analytical models. In this approach, metadata and semantic relationships between tables are stored as triples in SAP HANA Cloud KGE. We leverage ontologies to establish these semantic relationships, also stored as triples within SAP HANA Cloud.

When a business user submits a natural language query, the application identifies relevant semantic relationships, dynamically generates appropriate SQL, and presents a refined response using an LLM. While our demonstration uses database tables, this approach easily extends to OData services or Calculation Views.

Though the retrieval mechanism remains consistent across these scenarios, the triple generation approach differs significantly when working with tabular data by employing ontology-based modeling. Please refer to the complete mission for detailed implementation guidance and examples.

What About the Architecture for Unstructured Data Use Cases?

The architecture leveraging SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine and Large Language Models (LLMs) for unstructured data consists of two distinct phases: Triple Generation and Knowledge Retrieval. Let's examine each component:

Phase 1: Generating Triples (Steps 1-3)

  1. Document Processing & Chunking:
    PDF documents from either local FTP servers or cloud storage are accessed and systematically divided into manageable chunks. This chunking strategy is critical for efficient processing and ensuring the graph transformer can effectively analyze contextual relationships.

  2. Graph Transformation:
    The chunked text undergoes transformation through a graph transformer powered by Azure GPT-4o LLM. This sophisticated process parses and categorizes entities while identifying meaningful relationships between them, creating a structured representation of previously unstructured content.

  3. Triple Creation & Ingestion:
    The identified entities and relationships are converted into standardized RDF triples using RDF libraries. These triples are then ingested into SAP HANA Cloud via SPARQL insert operations, forming the foundation of our queryable knowledge graph.

Phase 2: Retrieving Triples Based on User Prompts (Steps 4-7)

  1. Validation Through User Interaction:
    The ingested triples can be validated through direct user prompts using either the provided Python scripts or a custom application included in the mission materials.

  2. Query Transformation:
    User prompts in natural language are transformed into precise SPARQL queries using specialized prompting templates, enabling accurate knowledge retrieval from the graph.

  3. Knowledge Graph Query Execution:
    The generated SPARQL query is executed against either the default namespace or a specified named graph, retrieving relevant triples that match the user's information needs.

  4. Response Refinement:
    Before presenting results to the user, we enhance the raw triple data using Anthropic Claude Sonnet (accessed through AWS Bedrock foundation models) to generate coherent, contextually relevant responses. This validation can be performed through the provided Python scripts or mission application.

This architecture demonstrates how unstructured document knowledge can be transformed into structured, queryable information that supports natural language interactions while leveraging the complementary strengths of knowledge graphs and large language models.

What About the Architecture Using LLMs from SAP GenAI Hub?

 

 

For our use case leveraging SAP HANA Cloud KGE and  LLMs from SAP GenAI Hub for unstructured data, we adopt a similar approach to what we discussed previously, with one key distinction.

Currently, we are utilizing SAP GenAI Hub exclusively for the retrieval phase (Steps 4-7) and not for the triple ingestion process (Steps 1-3). This means that while our knowledge retrieval, query transformation, and response generation leverage SAP GenAI Hub's capabilities, the initial document processing, graph transformation, and triple creation still rely on our previously described workflow.

As SAP continues to expand GenAI Hub's capabilities with relevant langchain implementations, we plan to update the mission to incorporate these advancements across the entire pipeline. This will eventually enable a fully integrated approach using SAP's native generative AI capabilities throughout the knowledge graph lifecycle.

We will keep the mission documentation current as these implementations become available, ensuring you have access to the most efficient and integrated approach possible.

What Configurations & Prerequisites Are Needed for This Discovery Mission?

Before embarking on this Knowledge Graph journey, you'll need to set up several key components to ensure a smooth experience throughout the Discovery Mission.

First and foremost, please review the comprehensive "Preparation" and "Setup" sections of the Project Board included in the Discovery Mission. These sections provide detailed guidance on configuring your environment properly.

 

Critical requirements include:

  • SAP HANA Cloud Triple Store Activation: Ensure you have enabled the triple store functionality on your SAP HANA Cloud instance. This is essential for storing and querying the RDF triples that form the foundation of the knowledge graph.

  • LLM Access for Graph Transformation: To generate high-quality triples using graph transformers, you'll need an Azure OpenAI subscription. This powers the sophisticated entity and relationship extraction process from unstructured documents.

  • Retrieval Layer Options: For the knowledge retrieval and response generation components, you have flexibility in your approach. You can leverage either:

    • SAP GenAI Hub (recommended for SAP-integrated environments)
    • Azure OpenAI services
    • AWS Bedrock subscription

Each option provides robust capabilities for transforming user queries and generating refined responses, allowing you to select the platform that best aligns with your existing technology stack or strategic direction.

The mission documentation provides specific connection parameters and configuration steps for each of these components to streamline your setup process.

What is the Mission implementation approach with SAP HANA Cloud KGE?

In our mission, we've curated a comprehensive set of scenarios that highlight the powerful capabilities of the SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine, SAP Generative AI Hub, and partner LLMs from AWS Bedrock and Azure OpenAI. For practitioners and technical experts looking to gain hands-on experience, we've developed detailed Python scripts that serve as practical implementation guides.

Scenario Coverage: 

Unstructured Data Knowledge Graphs (Scenarios 1-5)

Implementation Scenarios: Unstructured Data
These scenarios provide end-to-end workflows for transforming document-based information into queryable knowledge graphs. Each scenario clearly identifies which LLM service powers specific components and precisely where SAP HANA Cloud KGE plays a critical role in the processing pipeline.

Tabular Data Knowledge Graphs (Scenarios 6-7)

Our tabular data scenarios demonstrate how structured business data can be enhanced through knowledge graph representations. These examples illustrate ontology creation, semantic relationship modeling, and natural language interfaces for business data access.

To facilitate smooth implementation, we've included comprehensive setup guides for all required services:

  • Azure OpenAI configuration and API integration
  • AWS Bedrock foundation model access
  • SAP GenAI Hub connection setup
  • Authentication key management for Python script integration

 

These detailed instructions ensure you can focus on exploring the knowledge graph capabilities rather than troubleshooting configuration issues. The provided Python scripts serve not only as implementation tools but also as learning resources that can be adapted for your specific use cases.

How to Deploy the Knowledge Graph Application?

Deploying the Knowledge Graph application is straightforward—simply follow the comprehensive step-by-step instructions provided within the deployment tile of our mission. The application has been designed with flexibility in mind, offering full compatibility with LLMs from both SAP Generative AI Hub and AWS Bedrock, allowing you to leverage whichever platform best suits your organization's needs.

What's Coming Next

In our upcoming blog, we'll provide a practical, hands-on exploration and detailed code review for one of the key scenarios we've discussed. This deep dive will give you actionable insights into implementation specifics and best practices for working with knowledge graphs and LLMs in an integrated environment.

We strongly encourage you to engage with the mission content and follow the guided journey to gain a comprehensive understanding of SAP HANA Cloud KGE and its impressive capabilities. The hands-on exercises will help you build practical skills that can be applied to your specific business challenges.

Your feedback is invaluable to us! As you work through the mission, we welcome your thoughts, suggestions, and questions. Should you encounter any difficulties while exploring the knowledge graph implementation, our support team is readily available through the Discovery Center Mission support channels.

Here's to an enjoyable learning journey as you discover the transformative potential of combining SAP HANA Cloud knowledge graphs with LLMs!

entry.title: First Half 2025 Release: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central?
entry.content:
The First Half 2025 Release delivers many exciting innovations within SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central. We are introducing new features and capabilities designed to make finding information and completing tasks quick and easy. Here is a snapshot of what you can expect to see in this release:

People Profile Enhancements
Building upon a successful launch of our new People Profile, we are continuing to enhance the experience by bringing in additional content from across SAP SuccessFactors solutions, including benefits, payroll, and learning.

Additional Learning and Benefits Content in the People Profile


To further support the unique requirements of public sector organizations, we are also introducing a new portlet that displays the financing status of an employee directly within their profile.

Financing Status in the People Profile


Addressing Customer Influence Requests
We are pleased to address multiple customer influence requests with new capabilities designed to boost efficiency for all users. Managers can now create job requisitions directly from the My Team Positions view, reducing the need to navigate between pages. In addition, attachments can now be uploaded within quick actions to help ensure smooth task completion when further documentation is required.

Attachments in Quick Actions


To simplify the administrator experience, we are also introducing support for bulk changes to employee data within the mass data management tool, enabling non-technical users to quickly update job information, such as cost centers and holiday calendars.

Mass Changes to Job Information


These are just a few of the many innovations in the SAP SuccessFactors First Half 2025 Release. To learn more, check out the resources below:

  • Visit the SAP SuccessFactors Community for release documentation, product videos, and solution area blogs
  • Watch the 1H 2025 Release Highlights Overview Video, featuring Bianka Woelke, GVP Head of Application Product Management

entry.title: SAP CodeJam: Build AI Services with SAP CAP and Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
🚀Join Us for the Next SAP CodeJam: Build AI Services with SAP CAP and Generative AI Hub

Are you ready to take your SAP development skills to the next level with generative AI? Then don’t miss our upcoming SAP CodeJam focused on “Building AI Services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model and SAP Generative AI Hub.”

🗓 Date: Friday, April 25th, 2025
📍 Location: SAP America, Inc. – Newtown Square, PA
🕤 Time: 9:30 AM – 3:30 PM (in-person only)
🎯 Level: Intermediate to Advanced (CAP & JavaScript experience recommended)

💡 What You’ll Learn

This hands-on CodeJam is all about making AI real in your SAP applications. You’ll explore how to:

  • Integrate SAP Generative AI Hub with your own applications

  • Expose genAI capabilities via OData services built using SAP CAP

  • Use SAP AI Core & SAP AI Launchpad to manage and execute your AI scenarios

Work with foundation models and understand their impact in real business contexts

You’ll walk away with the skills to build and expose AI-powered services on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)—grounded in best practices and designed to scale.

🛠Hands-On Learning with Real Tools

Throughout the day, you’ll get your hands dirty with:

  • CDS modeling and service definitions

  • Integration patterns between CAP and AI services

  • Configuring and calling Generative AI APIs

Exploring prompt engineering for SAP-specific use cases
We’ll provide a guided environment, but you’ll be doing the building!

🧠  Who Should Attend?

This CodeJam is perfect for:

  • SAP Developers working with CAP and Node.js/JavaScript

  • Solution Architects exploring AI-driven applications

Anyone curious about the SAP Generative AI Hub and its potential

Basic knowledge of CAP and JavaScript is highly recommended, as the exercises will dive right into the good stuff.

👥 Let’s Build Together

CodeJams are a great way to connect, learn, and build with fellow SAP enthusiasts. Bring your laptop, your curiosity, and your questions—we’ll take care of the rest.

💻This is an in-person event only, so don’t forget to register early to reserve your spot!

Register here: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/build-ai-services-using-the-sap-cloud-application-programming-model-and-sap/ev-p/14068639

Let’s build the future of Business AI—together. See you in Newtown Square!

entry.title: Re: Adopting General Display Name
entry.content:
Thanks, @HarveyXu  - well, the way I originally represented circumstances has changed somewhat, likely due to a delay in the refresh of General Display Name on our staging environment. 

I do have an active case open with SAP support.  But for now, we essentially have the same problem if we want to maintain our status quo while General Display Name is enabled (which, of course, is mandatory for 1H 2025): 

The Desire: 

  1. Manage Users search results sort by FIRST M LAST in BizX
  2. Users search results sort by LAST, FIRST M in Learning Administration.  
  3. Avoid having to run some manual process to follow our automated user import processes from ERP to re-sort users in the preferred order in BizX/Learning

The Reality: 

  1. Enabling General Display Name on our production environment causes users in Learning to display in search results with FIRST M LAST format, counter to the status quo prior to General Display Name being enabled.
  2. Enabling General Display Name on our staging environment (where 1H 2025 is now deployed) does the same, but not in all cases...certain users still display LAST, FIRST M (the preferred method) while General Display Name is enabled.  Possibly because of the DISPLAYNAME value (or lack thereof) in the BizX-to-Learning import file, or possibly because we manually import users into our staging environment occasionally between instance refreshes.

entry.title: Re: pyodbc runs "select" queries very slowly when providi...
entry.content: Here's a SAP Note about a similar behavior, but with cached statements (automatically precompiled SQL statements) instead of prepared statements:

entry.title: Re: pyodbc runs "select" queries very slowly when providi...
entry.content:
Here's a SAP Note about a similar behavior, but with cached statements (automatically precompiled SQL statements) instead of prepared statements: https://me.sap.com/notes/2077161/E

Query takes a long time to complete due to bad table scan when statement cache or literal autoparam is on

ASE query showplan shows that optimizer is choosing a table scan instead of more efficient index scan when statement cache and literal autoparam are on.
Optimizer chooses the more efficient index scan when literal autoparam is off
Query contains multiple copies of the same literal value

Environment
SAP Adaptive Server Enterprise (ASE) 15.7
SAP Adaptive Server Enterprise (ASE) 16.0

Cause
When literal autoparam is used, the optimizer cannot assume that the literal values are all the same, as different values could be substituted for the next execution and the cached plan will need to be reasonable for both circumstances. When literal autoparam is not active, the optimizer can generate a better plan based on knowing the values of the literals.

The issue has been identified and logged under SAP CR 644531. Its description reads "When statement cache or literal autoparam is on, the query chooses a bad table scan that takes a long time to complete. With statement cache off, or literal autoparam off, the query chooses the index."

Resolution
There is currently no target Fix Pack or Service Pack scheduled for SAP CR 644531. It is being reviewed by our Product Group. Subscribe to this KBA to be automatically notified when SAP CR 644531is fixed.
Workarounds are:

set statement_cache off
or
sp_configure "literal autoparam", 0

entry.title: Re: pyodbc runs "select" queries very slowly when providi...
entry.content:
Ahhh, you updated your comment while I was typing mine.

-------------

In OP's case I'd opt for set statement_cache off; select ...; set statement_cache on.

Disabling configs (eg, literal autoparam) at the server level should be thoroughly tested as said changes could adversely affect other queries, users and/or applications. ("Duh, Mark!" ?)

entry.title: What is using logical memory in my ASE server?
entry.content:
My ASE server (16.0 SP04 PL04) reports it's using about 22.4 gbytes of memory (value of "total logical memory"), but the total of the memory config options is much less than that:

  • 6.8 gbytes data cache configured
  • 0.57 gbytes of procedure cache configured
  • statement caching disabled
  • abstract plan cache is configured to zero
  • heap memory per user is about 28.6 mbytes (only about 18 threads running including systems threads)

I found this nice diagram of things which use memory in ASE:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ASE/91d32d977a174c68829880bc020fc352/a99af2a8bc2b1014b832d6a1b32724c8.html?locale=en-US

So I think I should only be using 10 gbytes or less.  But ASE says it's using much more than that.  Is that just normal overhead?  How can I figure out what's using memory? 

Thanks in advance
Ben

entry.title: Erroneous Authentication Popup when Previewing Fiori Application in BAS
entry.content:
I'm a longtime ABAP developer who is currently learning Fiori development & I'm running into an issue that has me stumped. Below are the steps that I have taken to get to where I am now.

1. I am using S4HANA demo system A4H that is running in a docker container on my desktop computer.
2. In A4H, I created OData project ZFUM in SEGW, implemented the needed ABAP code, and exposed this on the gateway as service ZFUM_SRV.
3. In A4H's docker container, I'm also running a Cloud Connector instance.
4. I created a BTP Trial account and connected this trial account to the Cloud Connector instance running in the A4H docker container.
5. I created destination A4H in the BTP Cockpit using BasicAuthentication specifying the user and password of a user in A4H.
6. I subscribed to BAS in the BTP Cockpit and created a dev space in BAS.
7. In the BAS dev space, I created a basic freestyle Fiori project using the A4H destination and the ZFUM_SRV service.
8. I developed some views in the Fiori project using the OData service and I was successfully able to preview this application in BAS without being presented any authentication popups (since the user and password are specified in the BTP destination).
9. Ok, no problems yet. So, when I'm done for the day, I close BAS and shutdown the docker container running A4H and Cloud Connector.
10. The next day, I start the docker container with A4H and start the Cloud Connector. I start the dev space in BAS, but now when I try to preview the app, it is presenting the following authentication popup:

It should not be asking for user and password because that is specified in the BTP destination. I tried providing the same user & password the destination is using, but it does not work and the data is not fetched. The only solution I have found so far is to delete the dev space in BAS, create a new dev space, & clone the same Fiori development files from git. After I do this, then the preview works as it did the previous day and the OData calls are successful without seeing any authentication popup.

This workaround is fine for now I suppose, but it seems extreme to have to recreate a dev space every day. So, I assume there is something I am missing to prevent this issue. What can I do to prevent this problem or to more gracefully address it each time I see it happen?

entry.title: RAP - sending base64 content/document type from ABAP backend to Fiori Element Front-end?
entry.content:
Hey there. ABAP developer here that is slowly learning Fiori/RAP. I am building a RAP application in Eclipse using SAP Fiori Elements, that is based on a older SAP program we have using one underlying database table. I am trying to mimic some print to PDF functionality and my main question is how we can send a base64 file/file type from the ABAP backend to the fiori elements front end? 

In our current SAP program, clicking this particular print button will prompt the user to select a document to print for the selected table row data. Backend ABAP logic will then generate a JSON request -> REST call -> and in the JSON response we have some document binary data that is converted to Base64. We then use SAP "cl_gui_frontend_services" to download the file, usually in PDF format. Document then opens up on users screen. This is what I want to mimic in RAP. I currently have a OdataV4 service exposing my database table through CDS entities. I created a action event in my behavior definition that generates a "Print" button on the front-end.

So far my Print action event in my RAP app, I have the code in place to retrieve the binary data from the response of the REST call which is working correctly. Now I'am trying to work through sending the base64/file type of this RAP action event to the front-end so that the user can see the document. I'm currently doing some research on this, and have found some other articles on this particular subject, but most of them are for uploading files/attachements. I believe its the same functionality that I am looking for, but I wanted to ask if anybody has had any similar requirements? 

__PRESENT

__PRESENT

__PRESENT

__PRESENT

__PRESENT

Exchange Rate issue for Material Cost Estimates
This is the standard behavior of the system. At another customer, the exchange rate type P was only maintained once a year. If they want to update the exchange rate it is possible but the common practice is to have a frozen exchange rate type P with the date of 01/01/Current Year.

So basically, change the rate of P instead of switching to M. In his case, can they simply maintain P (because he mentioned they do not currently maintain it)?

 

 

For now, I will leave this question unanswered and hopefully someone else can reply.

entry.title: Code Inspector exception NO_ELEMENTARY_TYPE in CL_CI_VARIANT_CONFIGURATION
entry.content:
I have this exception when running a custom program with code as described in Print details of an ATC/code inspector check varia... - SAP Community, when it executes the public method VARIANT_TO_XML of CL_CI_VARIANT_CONFIGURATION. NO_ELEMENTARY_TYPE is a local exception class inheriting from CX_NO_CHECK.

By debug, the root cause seems to be in the method IF_CI_TEST~QUERY_ATTRIBUTES of the class CL_CI_TEST_EMPTY_UNUSED_PROCS, the parameter TEST_KEY is a ranges table but is defined with KIND = ' ' which indicates an elementary data type. It should be defined with KIND = 'S' which indicates a ranges table.

The issue happens with at least ABAP 7.56 and 7.58.

There's no fix in SAP notes.

I'd like to find a way to export a code inspector variant as XML by standard means, to reproduce that "bug" and make it fixed by SAP.

Do you know one standard way which calls the public method VARIANT_TO_XML of CL_CI_VARIANT_CONFIGURATION?

Or should I fix the KIND value by myself, or do you advise doing the export by full custom code, or contact SAP support anyway?

Thanks.

Sandra

entry.title: Create delivery with future stock
entry.content:
Hi,

I would like to create deliveries with future stock. The process is as below

1) sales order is created for FERT materials. As stock is not available, schedule lines are not confirmed.

2) with MRP run, PR is created.

3) PO is created from PR.

4) BOP job is executed.

5) with BOP run, schedule line is confirmed with future stock.

I would like to create delivery a few days in advance, so that warehouse folks can plan the logistics activities. However, when I tried creating delivery, I was getting error "Only 0 ST of material XXXXX available".

Also, when i was checking SAP help, I saw below:

*********************************************************************************************

Configuration for Deliveries

For all options of the Availability Check field (except the No PAC Check option), the product availability check for a delivery only considers stock and deliveries. The No PAC Check option confirms all requirements for the requested delivery date, as for sales orders.
*********************************************************************************************
Does this mean, we can't create deliveries with future stock?
 
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: pyodbc runs "select" queries very slowly when providi...
entry.content: Hey Ryan, thanks for that comment. That's given me a little bit of a lead--the file that Ribo translated says that my filename has been auto-translated to a "LONGBINARY" type. Indeed, when I run "select * from files where filename=convert(varchar(100),?)" as my query, the query runs near-immediately! So, the problem seems to be that pyodbc translates Python strings to that "LONGBINARY" type for whatever reason. That's a great lead!

Sign up now for the 2505 Early Release Series

Follow this blog to receive notification of new series as soon as registration opens. 

SAP strives to equip our customers and partners with valuable release readiness resources for each SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network quarterly release.

Early Release Series

The SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network Early Release series of webcasts cover features planned for the upcoming quarterly release.  This platform, available to customers and partners, has a clear mission: deliver an interactive forum to engage with SAP product experts providing simple, direct answers to your most pressing questions.  There is a different session for each product area with content tailored for the specific audience:

If you want to learn more and actively engage with SAP subject matter experts, we encourage you to participate in these informative sessions.  Register now!

Quarterly Release Communications

Notice of upcoming quarterly releases will be communicated to Buyer customers three weeks prior to the release date.  Suppliers receive notice two weeks prior.  Releases are communicated via email notification (look for subject line starting with: Advance Release Notice).

Anyone from your organization that has chosen to subscribe to product notifications will receive these announcements. 

The release calendar for SAP Business Network  and release calendar SAP Ariba Applications identify release timing several quarters in advance.

Where to find out more details?

entry.title: Re: Performance comparison: FILTER vs LOOP vs FOR
entry.content:
@Sandra_Rossigood point. I've quickly extended my code to run both LOOP and VALUE on variable and on fs. I'm a bit lazy, so I checked it only on sorted tables (results are at the end of the comment).

Truth be told, it doesn't change much. Field symbol improves the performance:

  • on Cloud, by 2-10% which is almost a measuring error tolerance (and that's a surprise for me)
  • on ECC, by solid 15-20%.

FOR remains slower than LOOP by 25-30% on ECC.

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Recife 2025: Tudo sobre a Programação e os Palestrantes #sitREC2025
entry.content:
O que esperar do SAP Inside Track Recife 2025?

Com o tema “It’s all about the people”, o SIT Recife 2025 reunirá especialistas que atuam diariamente em projetos SAP no Brasil e no exterior. A agenda foi cuidadosamente planejada para oferecer uma imersão em temas atuais como SAP S/4HANA, FIORI, BTP, Clean Core, Inteligência Artificial e muito mais.

 

Cada palestra foi selecionada com base em seu potencial de gerar valor imediato para os participantes — trazendo estudos de caso, experiências práticas e insights de mercado.

Agenda Confirmada

  • 08:00 – 08:30 | Credenciamento e Coffee-Break
  • 08:30 – 08:40 | Abertura Oficial
  • 08:40 – 09:10 | Marssel Vilaça – Estudo de caso com os Caminhos da Conversão para o S/4HANA
  • 09:10 – 09:50 | Fabio Dezan – Mitos e Verdades sobre a Nova Localização Brasil e o Fim da TAXBRA no S/4HANA Public Edition
  • 09:50 – 10:30 | Lucas Ferreira – Como extrair o melhor do FIORI com personalizações e Clean Core
  • 10:50 – 11:30 | Gabriel Magalhães – O Fim da SE38? O Futuro do Desenvolvimento ABAP no S/4HANA
  • 11:30 – 12:10 | Luciano Casado – Pagamento com cartões integrado ao SAP S/4HANA
  • 12:10 – 12:30 | Capgemini – Apresentação Institucional
  • 12:30 – 14:00 | Intervalo para Almoço
  • 14:00 – 14:40 | Antonio Figueredo – Sistema desenvolvido a partir do BTP para relacionamento com clientes
  • 14:40 – 15:20 | Manuel Robalinho – A Inovação na Comunicação Bancária com o SAP e o uso de IA em Finanças
  • 15:40 – 16:20 | Sérgio Ferreira – Desbravando o Futuro com SAP Analytics e Business Data Cloud
  • 16:20 – 17:00 | Edson Inowe – IA no Ecossistema SAP: Inovação, Eficiência e o Futuro da Gestão Empresarial
  • 17:00 – 17:30 | Encerramento + Sorteio de brindes

Interessado em palestrar em futuras edições?

Para esta edição, nossa agenda de palestras está completa, mas se você deseja ser notificado sobre os próximos eventos SAP Inside Track no Brasil, entre na nossa lista de interesse:

🔗Cadastre-se aqui para ser notificado

 

Um evento com propósito

Mais do que uma agenda técnica, o SIT Recife é um evento colaborativo — feito por membros ativos da Comunidade SAP com o objetivo de compartilhar conhecimento real, formar conexões profissionais e fortalecer o ecossistema local.

Organizado de forma independente e com o apoio de empresas comprometidas com a inovação, o evento mostra a força da comunidade e sua capacidade de transformar carreiras e negócios.

  • Data: 10 de Maio de 2025
  • Local: Kastel Manibu Recife Hotel - 919 Avenida Conselheiro Aguiar Boa Viagem, PE 51011-031
  • Hashtag oficial: #sitREC2025
  • Transmissão ao vivo: Youtube SAPInsideTrack

Prepare-se para um dia de muito conteúdo, conexões e inspiração.
Nos vemos no SAP Inside Track Recife 2025!

entry.title: Re: Error in Data Migration Cockpit for fixed asset data...
entry.content:
Hello Hirofumi

Pattern 1:
SAP Knowledge Base Article 3429989 states that only specific sub-ledger line item types allow proportional valuation entries during migration.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3429989?utm_source

Ensure that the sub-ledger line item type is included in the permitted values for proportional transfer entries.

You may refer to the SAP Help Portal below for a list of valid line item types and their applications in the "Fixed Asset—Postings" migration object.


https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3132044?utm_source

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/3c0dddd9424549fe9c46db30780c128c.html?utm_source

Pattern 2:
If you are encountering this error for assets acquired in the current fiscal year, it may be due to incorrect or missing acquisition dates in the system. Please refer to the KBA below
https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2937635?utm_source

However, verify that your migration template accurately sets the acquisition dates for the assets concerned to the current fiscal year. Additionally, ensure that the transaction types used for disposals are appropriate for current-year acquisitions.

All data entries in the migration templates should be thoroughly validated before proceeding with the migration to ensure compliance with SAP's requirements for fixed asset postings. Asset postings.

Secondly it is not a bug, this is a known system limitation, the Data Migration Cockpit (DMC) does not support the acquisition and disposal of assets in a single step when they occur within the same fiscal year.

Is Migration Possible?
Yes – fixed assets that are acquired and disposed of within the same fiscal year can be migrated, but the process must be divided and executed in two distinct steps.

SAP-Recommended Workaround:
Step 1: Migrate Acquisition Only
Use: Migration Object "Fixed Assets – Posting" in DMC

Include only:

- Acquisition value

- Acquisition date (must fall in the current fiscal year)

- Depreciation start date

Do not include: Disposal or transfer data

Relevant T-Codes for verification/checks

- AS03 – Display Asset Master

- AW01N – Asset Explorer

Step 2: Post Disposal Manually in the System
Once the acquisition migration is complete:

Use T-Code: ABAVN – Post Retirement by Scrapping
(or Fiori App: “Post Retirement” if using S/4HANA)

For revenue-related disposals:
Use T-Code: ABAON – Asset Retirement with Revenue

This manual step ensures:

- Proper transaction flow

- Accurate depreciation

- Gain/loss is correctly calculated in the system.

entry.title: Re: In S4 HANA Public Cloud where do I find Configuration...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, traditional GUI transaction codes such as OKYD (for Costing Versions) and OKEQ (for Controlling Versions) are unavailable. Instead, configuration is conducted through Self-Service Configuration UIs (SSCUI) or the Manage Your Solution app from your SAP Fiori launchpad.

To configure exchange rate types for Costing Version and Controlling Version 0, follow these steps:

1.Access the Configuration Environment;

  • Navigate to the Manage Your Solution app.
  • Select Configure Your Solution.

2. Configure Exchange Rate Types;

  • Search for the SSCUI “Check Exchange Rate Types”.
  • You can define or modify exchange rate types as required.

3.Assign Exchange Rate Types to Costing Versions;

  • Search for the SSCUI “Define Costing Versions”.
  • You can assign the appropriate exchange rate type to your costing versions in this configuration activity.

4.Assign Exchange Rate Types to Controlling Versions:

  • Search for the SSCUI “Define Versions”.
  • Here, you can assign exchange rate types to controlling versions, including version 0.

Please note that the availability of specific SSCUIs may depend on your system's configuration and user permissions. If you cannot locate these SSCUIs, consult with your system administrator to gain/permit you access.
